providerNotifications: Albumin is < 3.0 or total bilirubin is > 1.5 and if this is not known to the prescriber
New side effects from recent injection
New abnormal involuntary movements, excessive sedation, dizziness, agitation, delirium, fever, tachycardia, abnormal blood pressure, or any concerning symptoms

guidanceNote: DESC created guidance

idealSteps: Dose not yet due. Please consult the haloperidol decanoate early administration guidance.
Administer usual haloperidol decanoate injection **if the dose is 200 mg or less**, otherwise
If the dose is greater than 200 mg, consult provider *before* administering.
If symptoms of psychosis have returned or worsened, recommend oral haloperidol supplementation (consult provider for order).
Schedule a check-in with patient in **6–7 days** (near peak dose) to assess for haloperidol-related adverse effects.
providerNotifications: **Pre-injection:** Consult provider only if haloperidol decanoate dose is greater than 200 mg.
**Post-injection:** Notify provider of the situation and request an order for oral supplementation if needed.

idealSteps: Do not administer. Reinitiation is required. **Consult provider BEFORE any injection.**
providerNotifications: **Before any injection:** Consult provider in all cases.

idealSteps: Dose not yet due. Please consult the haloperidol decanoate early administration guidance.
Administer usual haloperidol decanoate injection.
Arrange for next injection in 4 weeks.
Administer usual haloperidol decanoate injection **if the dose is 200 mg or less**.
If the dose is greater than 200 mg, consult provider *before* administering.
If symptoms of psychosis have returned or worsened, recommend oral haloperidol supplementation (consult provider for order).
Schedule a check-in with patient in **6–7 days** (near peak dose) to assess for haloperidol-related adverse effects.
providerNotifications: **Pre-injection:** Consult provider only if haloperidol decanoate dose is greater than 200 mg.
**Post-injection:** Notify provider of the situation and request an order for oral supplementation if needed.

idealSteps: Do not administer. Reinitiation is required. **Consult provider BEFORE any injection.**
providerNotifications: **Before any injection:** Consult provider in all cases.

fields.label: Date of last Haloperidol Decanoate injection
Prior consecutive monthly Haloperidol Decanoate injections received

options.label: 1–3 injections (steady state not yet likely achieved)
4 or more injections (steady state likely achieved)

requiredFields.message: Please enter the date of last Haloperidol Decanoate injection.

changes.from: ["This may be given earlier with provider approval"]
changes.to: ["This may be given earlier with provider approval","ss"]

guidanceNote: This may be given earlier with provider approval
ss

internalNotes: Booster dose, optional additional dose between monthly dosing

idealSteps: A booster dose may be administered any time if these requirements are met:
The client wants the booster dose
At least 24 hours has passed since the last LAI-buprenorphine injection
The client continues to report use of unregulated fentanyl or other unregulated opioids during the present day
providerNotifications: If the client is reporting NO use of unregulated opioids, please consult a provider. The main risk in this situation is too much buprenorphine causing sedation.

internalNotes: Standard monthly tiers — same threshold structure as Brixadi monthly

idealSteps: The injection is not yet overdue (less than 3 weeks since last administration). Please consult the early administration guidance.
Administer the next injection, regardless of the level of unregulated opioid use.
If the patient reports active use of unregulated opioids, discuss a switch to Sublocade 300 mg and consult prescriber if the patient is interested.*
Conduct a fentanyl dependence assessment before administering:
Administer next injection if the client has **“minimal/no fentanyl dependence”, see definition below**, or if the client has taken at least 8 mg of sublingual buprenorphine in the last 24 hours.
Administer if they have “moderate fentanyl dependence” and are ok with the risk of some bup-induced withdrawal.
If the patient reports active use of unregulated opioids, discuss a switch to Sublocade 300 mg and consult prescriber if the patient is interested.*
providerNotifications: Consult prescriber if patient does not meet minimal/no, or moderate fentanyl dependence criteria.

idealSteps: Conduct a fentanyl dependence assessment before administering:
Administer next injection if the client has **“minimal/no fentanyl dependence”, see definition below**, or if the client has taken at least 8 mg of sublingual buprenorphine in the last 24 hours.
If the patient reports active use of unregulated opioids, discuss a switch to Sublocade 300 mg and consult prescriber if the patient is interested.*
providerNotifications: Consult prescriber if patient does not meet minimal/no fentanyl dependence criteria.

idealSteps: **Consult a prescriber in real-time** before administering. The goal is to avoid having the patient leave without guidance. You may consult the ORCA Center or medical director.
providerNotifications: Consult prescriber before any injection.

see definition below**, or if the client has taken at least 8 mg of sublingual buprenorphine in the last 24 hours.
If the patient reports active use of unregulated opioids, discuss a switch to Sublocade 300 mg and consult prescriber if the patient is interested.*
providerNotifications: Consult prescriber if patient does not meet minimal/no fentanyl dependence criteria.

idealSteps: **Consult a prescriber in real-time** before administering. The goal is to avoid having the patient leave without guidance. You may consult the ORCA Center or medical director.
providerNotifications: Consult prescriber before any injection.

internalNotes: Weekly injections (24 mg or 32 mg): 9-day window, then prescriber guidance required

idealSteps: The weekly Brixadi injection is not yet due. Please consult the early administration guidance.
providerNotifications: No provider notification needed.

idealSteps: Administer the weekly Brixadi injection. Missed weekly doses (24 mg or 32 mg) may be given up to **9 days** after the last injection per the standing order.
providerNotifications: No provider notification needed.

idealSteps: The weekly Brixadi injection is more than 9 days overdue.
providerNotifications: Contact prescriber for guidance before administering.
